name: Mary Ann's Enchiladas
recipeInstructions:
To make the sauce:
Evaporated milk (½ to ¾ c)
Velveeta cheese - 1 lb
Cream of chicken soup - 1 can
Green onion and sour cream dip (optional) - 1 small container
Pimiento, chopped (from a jar) - 1 small can
Black olives, sliced, drained (2-4 oz. can)
Corn tortillas - 18 pieces
Cooking oil - about ½ c
Prepare the filling:
Brown ground beef with salt and garlic until crumbly. Drain excess fat.
Mix in grated Longhorn cheese and set aside.
Prepare the sauce:
Melt Velveeta cheese on low heat, adding cream of chicken soup and evaporated milk (microwave is easiest). Combine well, then add black olives and pimientos. If using green onion dip, include it now. Set aside until cool.
Prepare the tortillas:
Dip each corn tortilla briefly in hot oil for a couple of seconds using tongs, then place on paper towels to drain. Repeat draining later if needed.
Assemble enchiladas:
Spoon about 2 tablespoons of meat mixture onto each tortilla, roll tightly with seam side down, and place in a large rectangular baking dish.
Top the enchiladas with Velveeta sauce. Bake uncovered at 350°F for about 30 minutes, checking to prevent burning. Remove when bubbly and lightly browned.
Serve:
For serving, cut into portions and enjoy alongside Spanish rice or another favorite side dish.
